mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings growth stalls, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns exceed zero in >60% of periods where UNRATE remains above 7.0, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization and supply-side momentum that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of weeks following positive INDPRO growth, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, rising inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 235.0, or the threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that immediately boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the series shows zero autocorrelation with short-term market direction.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening supply-side momentum and reduced corporate output, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at a 95% confidence level.
